title: Clathrotropis grandiflora
intro: Clathrotropis grandiflora is a plant species that belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Clathrotropis grandiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Clathrotropis grandiflora include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Clathrotropis grandiflora is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Dalbergieae.
